notes: CA uses an explicit 4-level hierarchy: Domain → Strand → Sub-Strand → Foundation. Side-by-side age columns ('Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)' vs. 'Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)') MUST each become a separate indicator.

elements.title: Initiative
elements.description: TODO — copy verbatim Early-column prose for Foundation 1.2

elements.title: Initiative
elements.description: TODO — copy verbatim Later-column prose for Foundation 1.2

regression_cases.description: Side-by-side Early/Later columns under any Foundation must each emit their own indicator. Same code, same title, different age_band, different description.
regression_cases.assertion: for every Foundation N.N in the document, the detector output contains >= 2 indicators with that code, and the set of age_band values across them includes both 'Early (3 to 4 ½ Years)' and 'Later (4 to 5 ½ Years)'.

regression_cases.description: Age-band labels must never appear in the title field; they go to age_band.
regression_cases.assertion: no detected indicator has a title containing 'Early (' or 'Later ('.

regression_cases.description: The 'a.', 'b.', 'c.' lettered examples beneath a Foundation are not indicators.
regression_cases.assertion: no detected indicator's source_text starts with a lone single-letter prefix like 'a.', 'b.', 'c.' followed by an example fragment.

regression_cases.description: Every indicator must trace back through sub_strand → strand → domain.
regression_cases.assertion: for every indicator, sub_strand_code, strand_code, and domain_code in the parser output are non-null and resolve to existing elements.
